checks.describe: the dedicated kwok-scale control plane is up and answering /healthz (kwokctl cluster, separate from core-internals)
the cluster has been grown to a meaningful node count (>= 500 fake nodes) — the fleet is cheap
enough pods exist to make a full list expensive (>= 1000) — the read-amplification load
events are counted in the etcd object board (apiserver_storage_objects) — the high-churn kind that outruns pods at scale (the OpenAI lesson)
the API server exposes its own cluster-scope LIST pods latency histogram — the server-side view that stays low while clients wait on payload
API Priority & Fairness priority levels exist (leader-election + node-high have their own shares) — the load shield that keeps critical traffic alive at scale
checks.run: kubectl --context kwok-kwok-scale get prioritylevelconfigurations -o name 2>/dev/null | tr '\n' ' '; echo
